params.description: Sticker sheet appears showing 3 rows of 4 stickers in a clear rectangular arrangement.

beats.text: In the Warmup, you saw equal groups lined up into a rectangle. See this sticker sheet? The stickers aren't bunched up in bags or scattered around. They're lined up. Every sticker has a spot. Let me show you what I notice.

elements.description: Sticker sheet showing 3 rows of 4 stickers in a clear rectangular arrangement.

beats.text: See how the stickers are lined up? They go across, and they go up and down. Everything lines up into a rectangle.

params.description: System briefly outlines the full rectangle shape around the sticker sheet.

elements.description: Sticker sheet showing 3 rows of 4 stickers. Rectangle outline visible around the array.

beats.text: When objects are arranged like this, lined up in a rectangle, with every spot filled, that's called an ARRAY.

elements.description: Sticker sheet showing 3 rows of 4 stickers in rectangular arrangement.

params.description: 3×4 array of stickers appears. 3 rows, 4 columns. Neutral state, no highlights.

beats.text: Now let me teach you the names for what you're seeing. Watch this.

params.description: Row 1 (top horizontal line of 4 stickers) highlights.

beats.text: See this line going across? That's a row. Rows go across from left to right.

params.description: Row 2 highlights, then row 3 highlights sequentially.

beats.text: Row 1, row 2, row 3. Three rows, and they all go across.

params.description: All row highlights clear. Array returns to neutral state.
Column 1 (left vertical line of 3 stickers) highlights.

beats.text: Now watch this direction. See this line going up and down? That's a column. Columns go up and down.

params.description: Column 2, column 3, column 4 highlight sequentially.

beats.text: Column 1, column 2, column 3, column 4. Four columns, all going up and down.

params.description: All column highlights clear. Array returns to neutral state.

beats.text: So think about the difference between rows and columns. How many rows that go across does this array have?

elements.description: 3×4 array of stickers in neutral state. No highlights active.

beats.text: How many rows? Rows go across.
3 rows — the lines that go across. So this array has 3 rows and 4 columns. An array is equal groups organized into rows and columns.

elements.description: 3×4 array of stickers in neutral state. No highlights active.
3×4 array of stickers in neutral state. multiple_choice tool active.

params.description: 2×6 dot array appears. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.

beats.text: Here's a new array, this time with dots. How many rows does this array have? Remember, rows go across.
How many rows? (Rows go across.)
2 rows. The lines going across.

elements.description: 2×6 dot array. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.
2×6 dot array. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.

beats.text: Now how many columns? Columns go up and down.
How many columns? (Columns go up and down.)
6 columns. The lines going up and down. So this array has 2 rows and 6 columns.

elements.description: 2×6 dot array. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.
2×6 dot array. 2 rows going across, 6 columns going up and down.

params.description: 3×5 dot array appears. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.
Equation Builder appears in expression mode: [___] × [___]. Tile palette available below.

beats.text: This array has 3 rows, and each row has 5 dots. So we can say: 3 rows of 5.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.
Equation Builder in expression mode: [___] × [___]. Tile palette available.

params.description: Rows highlight sequentially: row 1 (5 dots), row 2 (5 dots), row 3 (5 dots).

beats.text: Here's how that becomes a multiplication expression. Watch.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. Rows highlighted sequentially, animation complete.
Equation Builder in expression mode: [___] × [___]. Tile palette available.

beats.text: 3 rows of 5. The first number is how many rows: that's 3.

params.description: 3 tile places into first slot of Equation Builder.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.
Equation Builder shows [3] × [___]. First slot filled with 3.

beats.text: The second number is how many in each row: that's 5.

params.description: 5 tile places into second slot. Expression shows 3 × 5.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.
Equation Builder shows 3 × 5. Both slots filled.

beats.text: 3 rows of 5. That's just like saying 3 groups of 5, and you know equal groups means multiplication. 3 rows of 5 becomes 3 times 5.

elements.description: 3×5 dot array. 3 rows, each row has 5 dots.
Equation Builder shows 3 × 5. Both slots filled.

beats.text: Now here's a new array.

params.description: Equation Builder clears. Expression returns to [___] × [___].
3×5 array removed from screen.
New 4×2 dot array appears. 4 rows, each row has 2 dots.

elements.description: 4×2 dot array. 4 rows, each row has 2 dots.
Equation Builder cleared, showing [___] × [___]. Tile palette: 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9.

beats.text: Build the expression. The first number is how many rows, the second is how many in each row.
Build the expression for this array.
4 times 2. 4 rows of 2. The words match the expression.

elements.description: 4×2 dot array. 4 rows, each row has 2 dots.
Equation Builder shows 4 × 2. Both slots filled correctly.
4×2 dot array. 4 rows, each row has 2 dots. place_tile tool active.
Equation Builder showing [___] × [___]. Tile palette: 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9. place_tile tool active.

params.description: 2×5 dot array appears. 2 rows with 5 dots each.
Equation builder appears in expression mode. Two blank slots with multiplication symbol between them. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10.

beats.text: One more. Look at this array and build the expression for the rows.
Build the row expression for this array.

validator.description: Student placed 2 × 5, correct expression for rows

beats.text: 2 times 5. 2 rows of 5. You've got it.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array. 2 rows with 5 dots each.
Equation builder showing completed expression 2 × 5.
2×5 dot array. 2 rows with 5 dots each.
Equation builder in expression mode with two empty slots and multiplication symbol. Tile palette available.

params.description: 2×5 dot array appears on screen. 2 rows, 5 columns of dots.

beats.text: So far, you've been describing arrays by rows, the lines going across. But here's something interesting about arrays: there's another way to describe them.

params.description: Rows briefly highlight then fade.
Columns brighten and highlight.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array. Columns highlighted.

params.description: 2×5 dot array appears.
Equation Builder appears in expression mode with two blank slots.

beats.text: You just described this array by ROWS: 2 rows of 5. That gave us 2 times 5.

params.description: Rows highlight briefly in sequence.
Expression 2 × 5 appears in Equation Builder.
Expression 2 × 5 fades out.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array visible.
Equation Builder with empty slots.

beats.text: But watch what happens when I look at the COLUMNS instead.

params.description: Columns highlight sequentially: column 1, then column 2, column 3, column 4, column 5.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array with columns highlighted sequentially.
Equation Builder with empty slots.

beats.text: I see 5 columns. Each column has 2 dots. So I can also say: 5 columns of 2.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array visible.
Equation Builder with empty slots.

beats.text: 5 columns of 2 becomes 5 times 2. The first number is how many columns. The second number is how many in each column.

params.description: Expression 5 × 2 appears in Equation Builder.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array visible.
Equation Builder showing 5 × 2.

beats.text: Same array, but a different description which gives us a different expression. 2 rows of 5 gave us 2 times 5. 5 columns of 2 gives us 5 times 2. Both describe THIS array.

params.description: Both expressions display briefly: 2 × 5 and 5 × 2 with array between them.

elements.description: 2×5 dot array visible.
Equation Builder showing both expressions 2 × 5 and 5 × 2.

beats.text: The rule is: your DESCRIPTION determines your EXPRESSION. What you SAY about the array is what you WRITE. Let's try one.

params.description: 2×5 array removed.
Equation Builder changes to word description mode: ___ columns of ___.
4×3 dot array appears.

elements.description: 4×3 dot array visible.
Equation Builder in word mode with two blank slots: ___ columns of ___. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9.

beats.text: This array has 4 rows of 3. But what's the column description? Count the columns going up and down, then count how many in each column.
Describe this array by columns.

params.description: 3 columns of 4 appears in word description.

beats.text: 3 columns of 4. Now build the expression to match.

elements.description: 4×3 dot array visible.
Equation Builder showing 3 columns of 4. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9.
4×3 dot array visible.
Equation Builder in word mode with two blank slots: ___ columns of ___. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9. place_tile tool active.

params.description: Equation Builder changes to expression mode: ___ × ___. Word description 3 columns of 4 remains visible nearby. Same tile palette.

beats.text: Build the expression for 3 columns of 4.

params.description: Expression 3 × 4 appears in Equation Builder.

beats.text: 3 columns of 4. 3 times 4. The description matches the expression.

elements.description: 4×3 dot array visible.
Equation Builder showing 3 × 4.
4×3 dot array visible.
Equation Builder in expression mode with two blank slots: ___ × ___. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9. place_tile tool active.

params.description: 4×5 dot array appears.
Equation builder appears in expression mode. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9, 12, 15, 20.

beats.text: Check out this array. First, describe it by rows. How many rows, and how many in each row? Build the row expression.
Build the row expression for this array.
4 times 5 — 4 rows of 5.

elements.description: 4×5 dot array.
Equation builder showing 4 × 5.
4×5 dot array. place_tile tool active on equation builder.
Equation builder in expression mode. Empty slots ready for tile placement.

params.description: Equation builder clears.
Columns highlight on the array.

beats.text: Same array. Now describe it by columns: how many columns, how many in each column? Build the column expression.
Build the column expression for this array.
5 times 4 — 5 columns of 4. Same array, two different expressions.

elements.description: 4×5 dot array with columns highlighted.
Equation builder showing 5 × 4.
4×5 dot array with columns highlighted. place_tile tool active on equation builder.
Equation builder in expression mode. Empty slots ready for tile placement.

params.description: Ice cube tray array appears on left side. 2 rows of 6 ice cubes.
Dot array appears on right side. 2 rows of 6 dots.

beats.text: Look at these two pictures. On the left, an ice cube tray. On the right, a dot array. Same number of rows. Same number of columns. Whether it's ice cubes or dots, it's the same array. 2 rows of 6.

params.description: Rows highlight simultaneously on both ice cube tray and dot array.
Rows highlight on dot array simultaneously with ice cube tray.
Columns highlight simultaneously on both ice cube tray and dot array.
Columns highlight on dot array simultaneously with ice cube tray.
Ice cube tray fades and is removed from screen.
Equation builder appears below dot array in expression mode. Two blank slots with multiplication sign between them. Tile palette displays: 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10, 12.

beats.text: The math is the same no matter what fills the spots. Build the row expression.
Build the row expression for this array.

validator.description: Student placed 2 × 6, correct expression for row perspective

params.description: Tiles 2 and 6 lock into place. Expression shows 2 × 6.

beats.text: 2 times 6. Whether it's ice cubes or dots, the math is the same.

elements.description: Dot array. 2 rows of 6 dots.
Equation builder showing confirmed expression 2 × 6.
Dot array. 2 rows of 6 dots.
Equation builder in expression mode. Two blank slots with multiplication sign. Tile palette available. Student can place tiles.

params.description: 3×4 dot array appears. Same array from section 1.1.
Equation builder appears in equation mode. Blank slots for factors and product. Tile palette shows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10, 12, 14.

beats.text: Here's your chance to choose. You can describe this array by rows OR by columns. It's up to you. Then build the full equation, including the product.
First, choose whether you'd like to describe this array by rows or columns.
Choose if you'd like to describe this array using ROWS or COLUMNS.

elements.description: 3×4 dot array. Student has chosen rows or columns path.
Equation builder in equation mode. Blank slots awaiting tiles.

params.description: Word-mode equation builder appears: blank rows of blank items. Tile palette active.

beats.text: Now describe this array by words and build the equation.
Fill in the word equation and the number equation.
You described it by rows. 3 rows of 4. 3 times 4 equals 12.

elements.description: 3×4 dot array.
Word equation builder: 3 rows of 4 items filled.
Number equation builder: 3 × 4 = 12 filled.

params.description: Word-mode equation builder appears: blank columns of blank items. Tile palette active.

beats.text: Now describe this array by words and build the equation.
Fill in the word equation and the number equation.
You described it by columns. 4 columns of 3. 4 times 3 equals 12.

elements.description: 3×4 dot array.
Word equation builder: 4 columns of 3 items filled.
Number equation builder: 4 × 3 = 12 filled.

params.description: Equation builder appears in word specification template format.

beats.text: You can describe arrays by rows or by columns, and each description gives you a different expression. Now you're going to BUILD arrays yourself. Tell the computer what to make, and then find the answer.

elements.description: Equation builder in word specification template format.

params.description: Empty array build area. No rows or items visible yet.
Row builder panel appears at bottom. Row +/- buttons active. Items per Row +/- buttons dimmed.

beats.text: Now YOU get to build arrays. Watch how this works.

elements.description: Empty array build area.
Row builder panel. Row +/- active, Items per Row +/- dimmed.

beats.text: I want to make 3 rows of 5. I said ROWS first, so I build the rows first.

elements.description: Empty array build area.
Row builder panel. Row +/- active, Items per Row +/- dimmed.

beats.text: I add my first row.

params.description: Row + button highlights.
First empty row placeholder appears, a faint horizontal line indicating where the row will be. No dots yet.

elements.description: Array build area showing 1 empty row placeholder, no dots.
Row builder panel. Row +/- active, Items per Row +/- dimmed.

beats.text: Second row.

params.description: Second empty row placeholder appears below the first.

elements.description: Array build area showing 2 empty row placeholders, no dots.
Row builder panel. Row +/- active, Items per Row +/- dimmed.

beats.text: Third row. I've set up 3 rows but there are no dots yet. I need to add columns to fill them in.

params.description: Third empty row placeholder appears.
Row +/- buttons dim and disable. Items per Row +/- buttons activate.

elements.description: Array build area showing 3 empty row placeholders, no dots.
Row builder panel. Row +/- dimmed, Items per Row +/- active.

beats.text: Now I add items to each row. Watch what happens. Each + puts one dot in every row.

params.description: Items per Row + button highlights.
First column appears: one dot in each of the 3 rows, forming a vertical line at the left. 3 dots total.

elements.description: Array build area showing 3 rows with 1 dot each. 3 dots total.
Row builder panel. Row +/- dimmed, Items per Row +/- active.

beats.text: One dot in each row. That's 3 dots.

params.description: Second column appears. 2 dots in each row now. 6 dots total.

elements.description: Array build area showing 3 rows with 2 dots each. 6 dots total.
Row builder panel. Row +/- dimmed, Items per Row +/- active.

beats.text: Two dots in each row, 6 dots. Keep going.

params.description: Third column appears. 3 dots in each row. 9 dots total.
Fourth column appears. 4 dots in each row. 12 dots total.
Fifth column appears. 5 dots in each row. 15 dots total.

elements.description: Array build area showing complete 3 by 5 array. 3 rows with 5 dots each. 15 dots total.
Row builder panel. Row +/- dimmed, Items per Row +/- active.

beats.text: 3, 4, 5 in each row. 3 rows of 5. 15 dots. I built it.

params.description: Items per Row +/- buttons dim and disable.

elements.description: Complete 3 by 5 array. 3 rows with 5 dots each. 15 dots total.
Row builder panel. Both Row +/- and Items per Row +/- dimmed.

beats.text: Now I write the equation.

params.description: Equation builder appears in equation mode. Template shows blank times blank equals blank. Tile palette displays numbers 3, 4, 5, 8, 12, 15, 18, 20.
Guide places tiles 3, 5, 15 into the three slots in sequence.

elements.description: Complete 3 by 5 array. 3 rows with 5 dots each. 15 dots total.
Row builder panel. Both buttons dimmed.
Equation builder showing 3 x 5 = 15. All slots filled.

beats.text: 3 rows of 5. 3 times 5 equals 15. I planned it, built it, and found the answer. Your turn.

elements.description: Complete 3 by 5 array. 3 rows with 5 dots each. 15 dots total.
Row builder panel. Both buttons dimmed.
Equation builder showing 3 x 5 = 15. All slots filled.

params.description: Empty arrays build area appears.
Row builder panel appears at bottom. Row +/- active. Items per Row +/- dimmed.

beats.text: Build an array with 4 rows of 6. Start with the rows.
Build 4 rows of 6. Add the rows first.

beats.text: You've learned to describe arrays by rows or columns, write expressions for each description, and find products. Let's see what you know.
